sections.label: Section 3.345
sections.translation: Ah , my father , if only beneath Ilium ’ s walls you had been slain , slashed by some Lycian spearman ! Then you would have left a good name for your children in their halls ,

sections.label: Section 3.350
sections.translation: and in their maturity you would have made their lives admired by men . And in a land beyond the sea you would have found a tomb heaped high with earth , no heavy burden for your house to bear —

sections.label: Section 2.354
sections.translation: — Welcomed there below by your comrades

sections.label: Section 2.355
sections.translation: who nobly fell , a ruler of august majesty , distinguished even beneath the earth , and minister of the mightiest , the deities who rule in the nether world . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.360
sections.translation: For in your life you were a king of those who have the power to assign the portion of death , [*] and who wield the staff all mortals obey .

sections.label: Section 3.363
sections.translation: No , not even beneath the walls of Troy , father ,

sections.label: Section 3.365
sections.translation: would I wish you to have fallen and to be entombed beside Scamander ’ s waters among the rest of the host slain by the spear . I wish rather that his murderers had been killed by their own loved ones , just as they killed you ,

sections.label: Section 3.370
sections.translation: so that someone in a distant land who knew nothing of these present troubles should learn of their fatal doom .

sections.label: Section 372
sections.original: ταῦτα μέν , ὦ παῖ , κρείσσονα χρυσοῦ ,
sections.translation: In this , my child , your wish is better than gold . It surpasses great good fortune , even that of the supremely blesssed ; [*] for it is easy to wish .

sections.label: Section 375
sections.translation: But now the lash of this double scourge [*] comes home : our cause already has its champions beneath the earth , while the hands of our loathsome opponents , though they have the mastery , are unholy . The children have won the day .

sections.label: Section 4.380
sections.translation: This has pierced the earth and reached your ear [*] as if it were an arrow . O Zeus , O Zeus , who send long-deferred retribution up from below onto the reckless and wicked deeds done by the hands of mortals . . . .

sections.label: Section 4.385
sections.translation: And yet it will be accomplished for our father ’ s sake . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.386
sections.translation: May it be mine to raise a hearty shout in triumph over the man when he is stabbed and over the woman as she perishes ! Why should I try to keep hidden what nevertheless hovers before my soul ?

sections.label: Section 5.390
sections.translation: Full against the prow of my heart wrath blows sharply in rancorous hate .

sections.label: Section 4.395
sections.translation: and split their heads open ? Let it be a pledge to the land ! After injustice I demand justice as my right . Hear , O Earth , and you honored powers below !

sections.label: Section 400
sections.translation: And it is the eternal rule that drops of blood spilled on the ground demand yet more blood . Murder cries out on the Fury , which from those killed before brings one ruin in the wake of another .

sections.label: Section 6.405
sections.translation: Alas , you sovereign powers of the world below , behold , you potent Curses of the slain , behold the remnants of the line of Atreus in their helpless plight , cast out from house and home in dishonor . Which way can we turn , O Zeus ?

sections.label: Section 5.410
sections.original: πέπαλται [*] δ αὖτέ μοι φίλον κέαρ
sections.translation: But again my heart throbs as I hear this pitiful lament . At once I am devoid of hope and my viscera are darkened at the words I hear .

sections.label: Section 5.415
sections.original: ὅταν δ αὖτ ἐπ ἀλκῆς ἐπάρῃ μ
sections.translation: But when hope once again lifts and strengthens me , it puts away my distress and dawns brightly on me .

sections.label: Section 6.418
sections.original: τί δ ἂν φάντες [*] τύχοιμεν ; ἢ [*] τάπερ
sections.translation: To what could we more fittingly appeal than to those very miseries we have endured from the woman herself who bore us ?

sections.label: Section 6.420
sections.original: πάρεστι σαίνειν , τὰ δ οὔτι θέλγεται .
sections.translation: She may fawn upon us , but they are past all soothing . For like a fierce-hearted wolf the temper we have acquired from our mother is implacable .

sections.label: Section 7.423
sections.original: ἔκοψα κομμὸν Ἄριον ἔν τε Κισσίας [*]
sections.translation: On my breast I beat [*] an Arian [*] dirge in just the same fashion as a Cissian [*] wailing woman .

sections.label: Section 7.425
sections.translation: With clenched fists , raining blows thick and fast , my outstretched hands could be seen descending from above , from far above , now on this side , now on that , till my battered and wretched head resounded with the strokes .

sections.label: Section 8.429
sections.translation: Away with you , cruel

sections.label: Section 8.430
sections.translation: and utterly brazen mother ! You dared to give your husband a most cruel burial : unmourned , without lamentation , a king unattended by his people .

sections.label: Section 9.434
sections.translation: Ah me , your words spell utter dishonor .

sections.label: Section 9.435
sections.translation: Yet with the help of the gods , and with the help of my own hands , will she not atone for the dishonor she did my father ? Let me only take her life , then let me die !

sections.label: Section 9.439
sections.original: ἐμασχαλίσθη [*] δέ γ , ὡς τόδ εἰδῇς , [*]
sections.translation: Yes , and I would have you know he was brutally mangled . [*]

sections.label: Section 9.440
sections.original: ἔπρασσε δ ᾇπέρ [*] νιν ὧδε θάπτει ,
sections.translation: And even as she buried him in this way , she acted with intent to make the manner of his death a burden on your life past all power to bear . You hear the story of the ignominious outrage done to your father .

sections.label: Section 7.445
sections.original: λέγεις πατρῷον μόρον : ἐγὼ δ ἀπεστάτουν
sections.translation: My father was murdered just as you say . But all the while I was kept sequestered , despised , accounted a worthless thing . Kennelled in my room as if I were a vicious cur , I gave free vent to my streaming tears , which came more readily than laughter , as in my concealment I poured out my lament in plentiful weeping .

sections.label: Section 7.450
sections.original: τοιαῦτ ἀκούων ἐν φρεσὶν γράφου , πάτερ . [*]
sections.translation: Hear my tale and inscribe it on your heart .

sections.label: Section 8.451
sections.translation: Yes , let it sink deep into your ears , but keep inside a quiet steadfastness of soul . So far things are so . But you yourself be eager to resolve what is to follow .

sections.label: Section 8.455
sections.translation: You must enter the contest with inflexible wrath .

sections.label: Section 10.456
sections.original: σὲ τοι λέγω , ξυγγενοῦ , πάτερ , φίλοις . [*]
sections.translation: Father , I call on you ; side with your loved ones !

sections.label: Section 10.457
sections.translation: And I in tears join my voice to his .

sections.label: Section 10.458
sections.translation: And let all our company blend our voices to echo the prayer . Hear ! Come to the light !

sections.label: Section 10.460
sections.translation: Side with us against the foe !

sections.label: Section 10.461
sections.translation: Ares will encounter Ares ; Right will encounter Right .

sections.label: Section 10.462
sections.translation: O you gods , judge rightly the plea of right !

sections.label: Section 10.463
sections.translation: A shudder steals over me as I hear these prayers . Doom has long been waiting ,

sections.label: Section 10.465
sections.translation: but it will come in answer to those who pray .

sections.label: Section 11.466
sections.translation: Ah , inbred trouble and bloody stroke of ruin striking a discord ! Ah , lamentable and grievous sorrows !

sections.label: Section 11.470
sections.translation: Ah , the unstaunched pain !

sections.label: Section 11.471
sections.translation: Our house has a cure to heal these woes , a cure not from outside , from the hands of others , but from itself , by fierce , bloody strife .

sections.label: Section 11.475
sections.original: θεῶν τῶν [*] κατὰ γᾶς ὅδ ὕμνος .
sections.translation: This hymn is for the gods beneath the earth .

sections.label: Section 476
sections.translation: O you blessed powers below , hear this supplication of ours , and with a favorable will send forth to these children your aid for victory !

sections.label: Section 479
sections.translation: O father , who perished by a death unbefitting a king ,

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original: αἰτουμένῳ [*] μοι δὸς κράτος τῶν σῶν δόμων .
sections.translation: grant in answer to my prayer the lordship over your halls !

sections.label: Section 481
sections.original: κἀγώ , πάτερ , τοιάνδε [*] σου χρείαν ἔχω ,
sections.translation: And I too , father , have a like request of you : to escape when I have wrought great destruction on Aegisthus .

sections.label: Section 483
sections.original: οὕτω γὰρ ἄν σοι δαῖτες ἔννομοι βροτῶν
sections.translation: Yes , for then the customary funeral feasts of men would be established in your honor . But otherwise , at the rich and savory banquet

sections.label: Section 485
sections.translation: of burnt offerings made to the earth , you will be without a portion of honor .

sections.label: Section 486
sections.original: κἀγὼ χοάς σοι τῆς ἐμῆς παγκληρίας
sections.translation: And I will likewise at my wedding offer libations to you out of the fullness of my inheritance from my father ’ s house , and before all else I will hold this tomb of yours in the highest honor .

sections.label: Section 489
sections.original: ὦ Γαῖ , ἄνες μοι πατέρ [*] ἐποπτεῦσαι μάχην .
sections.translation: O Earth , send up my father to watch my battle !

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original: ὦ [*] Περσέφασσα , δὸς δέ γ εὔμορφον κράτος .
sections.translation: O Persephone , grant us indeed a glorious victory !

sections.label: Section 491
sections.translation: Father , remember the bath where you were robbed of life .

sections.label: Section 492
sections.translation: And remember how they devised a strange net to cast about you .

sections.label: Section 493
sections.translation: You were caught , my father , in fetters forged by no smith ’ s hand .

sections.label: Section 494
sections.translation: And in a fabric shamefully devised .

sections.label: Section 495
sections.translation: Father , are you not roused by taunts such as these ?

sections.label: Section 496
sections.original: ἆρ ὀρθὸν αἴρεις φίλτατον τὸ σὸν κάρα ;
sections.translation: Are you not raising that dearest head of yours ?
